us_equities.prose: Stocks rebounded broadly after the United States and Iran signed an interim agreement to wind down the war, which analysts described as a major positive signal for equity markets.

cited_sources.title: U.S. Stocks Poised to Close Week on Strong Note

rates_and_dollar.prose: The dollar rose and Treasury yields were supported after the Federal Reserve held rates unchanged in a unanimous decision at Kevin Warsh's first meeting as chairman, while traders began pricing growing odds of a rate hike later in the year.

cited_sources.title: Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise on First Fed Move Under Warsh

commodities.prose: Oil fell sharply after Trump signed a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing supply disruption fears, with Saudi tankers immediately resuming transit through the strait signaling a rapid normalization of crude flows. Gold retreated as geopolitical risk receded alongside the Iran agreement.

cited_sources.title: Oil Prices Fall to Lowest Since War Began After Trump Signs Iran Deal

volatility_breadth.prose: Fear gauges eased as the Iran deal reduced the geopolitical uncertainty that had been driving hedging demand, with oil supply fears cooling and gasoline prices falling in response to the Strait of Hormuz reopening.

cited_sources.title: Gas prices fall below $4 per gallon as oil supply fears ease after Iran deal
